pick.rationale: Nebraska holds a commanding ~24-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Troy (KenPom #14, AdjEM +26.12 vs. KenPom #143, AdjEM +1.74), with the 7th-best defense in the country nationally (Torvik AdjDE 93.47) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize a Troy offense ranked 136th nationally. The Cornhuskers' 26-6 record was forged against elite Big Ten competition — a level of schedule-strength battle-testing that Troy's Sun Belt profile (22-11) cannot replicate — and the betting market's heavy Nebraska line reflects broad consensus on the efficiency gap. Troy won back-to-back Sun Belt titles and is a credible automatic qualifier, but their defense ranked 181st nationally creates no viable path to containing Nebraska's offense, and the ~24-point efficiency chasm is historically insurmountable in single-elimination play. Nebraska's 0-8 all-time tournament record is a narrative footnote rather than a statistical predictor, and this matchup profile makes Troy one of the least credible 13-seed upset scenarios in the bracket.
